free_text: IN THE U-235 FISSION NEUTRON FIELD IN MOL.
.THE SAMPLES WERE DISK-SHAPED, HIGH PURITY METALLIC
FOILS, 10 MM IN DIAMETER AND BETWEEN .125 AND .5 MM
.THE SAMPLES WERE SANDWICHED BETWEEN TWO FOILS OF NI
OR IN, AND SEVERAL SANDWICHES WERE STACKED TOGETHER
TO A MAXIMUM THICKNESS OF 4 MM DURING IRRADIATIONS.
TWO DIFFERENT GE(LI) DETECTOR SYSTEMS WERE
USED, ONE IN MOL FOR SHORT-LIVED PRODUCTS AND THE
OTHER AT PTB FOR PRODUCTS WITH LONGER HALF-LIVES.
.A U-235 DOUBLE FISSION CHAMBER WAS USED TO MONITOR
THE FLUENCE RATE OF THERMAL NEUTRONS IN THE REACTOR
.THE REACTION RATES WERE CORRECTED FOR STRUCTURAL
MATERIAL PERTURBATIONS IN THE FISSION NEUTRON
CALCULATIONS. THESE CALCULATIONS MADE USE OF
ENDF/B-IV CROSS SECTIONS IN A 171 GROUP STRUCTURE
.CORRECTIONS WERE ALSO APPLIED FOR BACKSCATTERING OF
NEUTRONS FROM THE IRRADIATION CAVITY WALLS.
THE TOTAL ERRORS ARE COMPOSED OF-
ERROR IN THE RELATIVE PHOTOPEAK EFFICIENCY
